title: amaranthus viscidulus
intro: Amaranthus viscidulus, also known as sticky amaranth, is a plant species in the amaranth family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Amaranthus viscidulus include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as supporting cardiovascular health and immune function. The plant can be prepared as a tea or tincture for its medicinal properties, and it may also be used topically to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing.
